title: PM Shram Yogi Maandhan
meta_description: PM Shram Yogi Maandhan provides ₹3,000/month pension after age 60 for unorganized workers with monthly contribution of ₹55-200.

hero.one_liner: Guaranteed monthly pension of ₹3,000 after age 60 for unorganized sector workers with government matching contribution

what_is_it: PM Shram Yogi Maandhan (PM-SYM) launched in February 2019 is a voluntary pension scheme for unorganized sector workers. It guarantees ₹3,000 per month pension after the subscriber turns 60.

The scheme works on a 50:50 contribution model — whatever amount you contribute monthly, the government contributes an equal amount. Monthly contribution ranges from ₹55 (if you join at 18) to ₹200 (if you join at 40). After the subscriber's death, the spouse receives 50% of the pension (₹1,500/month).

eligibility.label: Income
eligibility.value: Monthly income must be ₹15,000 or less

eligibility.label: Occupation
eligibility.value: Unorganized sector — street vendors, domestic workers, rickshaw pullers, construction workers, etc.

eligibility.label: Exclusion
eligibility.value: Must NOT be member of EPFO, ESIC, NPS, or any other government pension scheme

eligibility.label: Not an income taxpayer
eligibility.value: Must not be paying income tax

how_to_apply.title: Visit nearest CSC center
how_to_apply.description: Carry Aadhaar card, bank passbook, and mobile phone. The CSC operator will help you enroll.

how_to_apply.title: Complete enrollment
how_to_apply.description: CSC operator fills the form, verifies Aadhaar, sets up auto-debit for monthly contribution.

how_to_apply.title: Start contributing
how_to_apply.description: Monthly contribution (₹55-200 based on your age) auto-debited from bank account. Government matches your contribution equally.

important_alert.text: If you stop contributing midway, you can either: (1) resume later by paying arrears with interest, (2) exit and get back only your contribution with savings account interest, or (3) wait till 60 and get a proportionately reduced pension.

faqs.question: How much do I pay per month?
faqs.answer: Depends on age at joining. Age 18: ₹55/month. Age 25: ₹80/month. Age 30: ₹105/month. Age 35: ₹150/month. Age 40: ₹200/month. Government matches every rupee.

faqs.question: Is ₹3,000/month pension enough?
faqs.answer: It's modest but remember — this is in addition to any other savings you have. With government matching your contribution, you effectively double your money. It's better than having no pension at all.

faqs.question: What happens if I die before 60?
faqs.answer: Your spouse can either continue contributing and get the full pension at 60, or exit and receive the accumulated corpus with interest.

faqs.question: Can I get both APY and PM-SYM?
faqs.answer: No. You can enroll in only one of these schemes. Choose APY if you want flexibility in pension amount (₹1K-5K). Choose PM-SYM if you want the government matching contribution benefit.
